subject: Public Health
title: The Antonine Plague
description: A 60-second lesson on The Antonine Plague.

hook.text: Roman soldiers returning from Mesopotamia in 165 CE brought home more than victory—they brought plague.

buildup.text: The disease—likely smallpox—spread across the Roman Empire, killing up to 2,000 people per day in Rome.

discovery.text: Galen, the famous physician, documented symptoms: fever, diarrhea, and a distinctive skin rash.

twist.text: The plague killed Emperor Marcus Aurelius' co-ruler Lucius Verus and decimated the Roman army.

climax.text: An estimated 5 million died. The weakened empire never fully regained its former military strength.

punchline.text: Rome's armies conquered the world. A virus conquered Rome.

quiz.question: What disease likely caused the Antonine Plague?
